claim_bullets.bullet_text: JavaScript allows rebinding values to parameters, but not to names bound with const (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.148-157).
const prevents rebinding of names, while let allows rebinding, both supporting block scoping (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.148-157).
var is function-scoped, not block-scoped, and hoisted to the top of the function, unlike let and const (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.148-157).

source_record_text: This chapter from JavaScript Allongé discusses rebinding and reassignment in JavaScript, focusing on how different declaration keywords (`const`, `let`, `var`) handle variable bindings and scoping.
